positiveClaims: The existing release claim command surface admission path is product-bound as bounded internal Review Bridge 12L admission-only evidence.
Accepted 12L requires the command id cmd.project.releaseClaim.admit to be present in the command surface kernel allowlist.
Accepted 12L requires bus-route command surface dispatch; direct route bypass remains blocked.
Accepted 12L calls the already-bound 12K execution gate and treats the 12K result as admission-only evidence.
Accepted 12L rejects non-plain or inherited top-level payload objects before invoking the 12K execution gate.
Accepted 12L emits deterministic non-mutating admission-only output by returning the bounded 12K execution-gate result without command execution.

This contour is command surface admission-only evidence; it is not command availability, command execution, release execution completion, product publication, release readiness, import, export, apply, storage truth, or project truth.
nonClaims: No release execution completion is claimed.
No release publication completion is claimed.
No release-wide publication authority is claimed.
No Google Docs support is claimed.
No DOCX support expansion is claimed.
No review import completion is claimed.
No manuscript write is performed by release claim command surface admission binding.
No project truth write is performed by release claim command surface admission binding.
No receipt or recovery evidence is created by release claim command surface admission binding.
No DOCX import safe-create behavior is changed.
No DOCX export behavior is changed.
No import/export MVP closeout is widened.
No PDF, EPUB, HTML, Mindmap, cloud sync, cross-platform readiness, packaged readiness, or Y9 admission is claimed.
layerDecisions: Command surface admission binding is admission-only chain evidence.
Command surface admission binding does not execute release commands.
Command surface admission binding does not prove command execution.
Command surface admission binding does not admit command availability.
Command surface admission binding does not admit release execution completion.
Command surface admission binding does not publish anything.
Command surface admission binding does not become project truth by itself.
12K execution gate remains upstream evidence and is not reopened semantically by this contour.
Real command execution remains a separate future owner-approved contour.
USER_FACING_CLAIM_READY remains an internal chain class only, not a release readiness state.
USER_FACING is a chain surface in this contour, not a user-facing UI state.
This contour is not a real release execution contour.
This contour is not a product publication contour.

machineChecks.command: node --test command surface admission wiring plus 12L binding contracts
node --test review bridge release claim binding contracts

agentAudits.finding: Confirmed 12L is canonically plausible only as the next explicit owner-approved bounded contour; it must not claim command availability, command execution, release execution completion, product publication, release readiness, user-facing release, import/export widening, apply, storage, receipt, recovery, dependency, or Y9.

Confirmed 12L is a thin command-surface wrapper over 12K; stale, synthetic, mutation, and inherited evidence risks are mostly upstream in 12K, while the practical 12L gap was top-level non-plain or inherited payload admission before 12K.

nextStep: select the next owner-approved boundary explicitly; real command execution, real product publication, and post-MVP import/export expansion do not open by implication
